# ellmos ControlCenter MCP

> MCP control plane for local MCP stack discovery, Claude profile inspection, capability bundle recommendation, policy audits, i18n-aware outputs, and a local dashboard.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/ellmos-controlcenter-mcp
npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/ellmos-controlcenter-mcp
MCP registry name: io.github.ellmos-ai/ellmos-controlcenter-mcp
License: MIT
Language: TypeScript
Runtime: Node.js >=18

## Summary

ellmos ControlCenter MCP is an alpha-stage Model Context Protocol server for local MCP administration. It discovers local MCP repositories, reads Claude profile files, resolves inherited profile configurations, recommends profiles and capability bundles for tasks, runs initial profile policy audits, builds local server catalogs, probes real MCP tool lists from local repositories or profiles, assigns tools to capability bundles, supports maintained output text sets for `de`, `en`, `es`, `zh`, `ja`, and `ru`, and can start a local dashboard.

It is useful for local MCP stack visibility and profile management. It is not a hardened security gateway. Gateway mode, enforced tool-level permissions, authentication, and hard isolation are planned but not implemented.

## Tools

- `controlcenter_status`: show stack, profile, and detected-server status.
- `controlcenter_get_language`: show the current ControlCenter output language.
- `controlcenter_set_language`: set the ControlCenter output language for this running server instance.
- `controlcenter_list_local_servers`: scan local MCP repositories below the configured MCP root.
- `controlcenter_list_tools`: start local or profile-defined MCP servers and read their real `list_tools` output.
- `controlcenter_assign_tool_bundles`: assign probed MCP tools to capability bundles.
- `controlcenter_list_bundles`: group local servers by capability bundle.
- `controlcenter_suggest_bundles`: recommend capability bundles for a task.
- `controlcenter_list_profiles`: list Claude profile files.
- `controlcenter_suggest_profile`: recommend a Claude profile for a task.
- `controlcenter_resolve_profile`: resolve profile inheritance, removals, and disabled servers.
- `controlcenter_switch_profile`: generate a resolved `--mcp-config` file and launch command.
- `controlcenter_audit_profile`: run initial profile policy checks.
- `controlcenter_build_catalog`: build a JSON catalog of local MCP servers, optionally including tool probes.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g ellmos-controlcenter-mcp
ellmos-controlcenter
```

Dashboard:

```bash
ellmos-controlcenter-dashboard
```

Default dashboard URL: http://127.0.0.1:3737

## Audience

- Developers managing local Claude Code MCP stacks who want visibility without manual JSON editing
- AI assistants (Claude, Codex, Gemini) that need to discover, catalog, or switch local MCP profiles programmatically
- Teams maintaining multiple MCP server setups and Claude profiles across projects
- Developers building on the ellmos-ai ecosystem or integrating FileCommander, CodeCommander, or n8n Manager
- Power users who want profile-aware policy audits and capability-bundle recommendations for their local AI tooling

## Search Phrases

- ellmos ControlCenter MCP local stack discovery
- MCP control plane Claude profile management
- local MCP server catalog dashboard
- capability bundle recommendation MCP
- Claude profile switch mcp-config generator
- MCP profile audit policy rules
- ellmos-ai MCP server TypeScript
- local MCP tool list probe
- ellmos controlcenter npm

## Important Files

- `README.md`: full English documentation.
- `README_de.md`: German documentation.
- `server.json`: MCP registry metadata.
- `data/capability-bundles.json`: default capability bundle definitions.
- `data/policy-rules.json`: default profile audit policy rules.
- `src/index.ts`: MCP server entrypoint and tool definitions.
- `src/i18n/`: ControlCenter text sets and language switching.
- `src/toolCatalog.ts`: MCP client probes for real server tool lists.
- `src/bundles.ts`: capability bundle definitions, suggestions, and tool assignment helpers.
- `src/dashboard.ts`: local dashboard server.
- `test/catalog.test.ts`: unit coverage for catalog, profiles, bundles, and policy rules.

## Last-checked: 2026-06-10
